Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 5 Airlines With The Largest Narrowbody Fleets In The United States

American, United, Delta, Southwest, and Alaska lead the US narrowbody fleet counts with collectively over 3,500 single-aisle aircraft.

The dominance of five carriers controlling 3,500+ narrowbody jets reflects significant industry consolidation, limiting competitive pressure on domestic route pricing and aircraft utilization strategies.

Switlik: Built for Survival

For more than a century, Switlik Survival Products has engineered customizable aviation survival solutions trusted by pilots and first responders worldwide.

Switlik's century-long specialization in customizable survival gear positions it as a critical supply chain partner for aviation operators managing liability and regulatory compliance requirements.

The image suggests general aviation operations at a facility where aircraft queue for runway access or services, illustrating typical traffic flow patterns at busy airfields.

Military Mar 31, 2026

Pentagon Considers Anti-Drone Lasers in Washington

Reported drone activity near Fort McNair has prompted review of counter-UAS system.

Military counter-UAS laser systems deployment near the capital signals escalating regulatory scrutiny that could reshape airspace management protocols for both civilian and defense operators.

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS represents a fundamental shift from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen.

ASU's experience-based training methodology establishes measurable performance benchmarks that other night vision programs now benchmark against for pilot competency validation.

Gone After Just 1 Year: Virgin Atlantic Axes This Long-Haul Route From London

Virgin Atlantic’s Riyadh exit looks abrupt, but the bigger story is where the aircraft goes next.

Virgin Atlantic's route withdrawal signals shifting capacity allocation as airlines reassess network profitability in competitive long-haul markets.

Military Mar 31, 2026

How Much Does A Fighter Jet Cost To Fly Per Hour?

A closer look at the true price of military air power.

Fighter jet operating costs exceed $20,000-$30,000 hourly when factoring fuel, maintenance, and personnel, revealing why air superiority demands sustained budgetary commitment.

TSA Paychecks Ease Airport Delays

Security lines shorten at several major airports, though the shutdown continues to raise staffing and pay concerns.

TSA staffing stabilization during shutdown demonstrates that pay continuity can mitigate operational disruption, but long-term retention risks remain unresolved for airports dependent on consistent security throughput.

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document why they accept residual risks after controls, not just prove controls work, shifting SRM focus from theoretical analysis to practical accountability.

Godspeed, Artemis II!

NASA astronaut Jessica Meir took this photo of an Artemis program patch floating in the International Space Station’s cupola. She posted it on X on March 30, 2026, with the following caption: “Our work on the @Space_Station has provided the foundation to explore further, preparing us to return...

ISS research directly enables lunar exploration capability, demonstrating how orbital operations validate technologies and procedures essential for deep-space missions.

International Mar 31, 2026

Jet fuel crisis deepens as Trump tells the world ‘Go get your own oil’

As airlines across the globe grapple with a serious jet fuel price crisis that has sent costs soaring…...

Trump's energy nationalism policy signals reduced U.S. fuel exports, forcing international carriers to secure alternative suppliers and likely locking in higher long-term jet fuel contracts.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

EASA: Narrow Mideast Corridors Increase Operational Risk

European regulators say conflict-related airspace restrictions are increasing congestion and risks.

Compressed routing through limited Middle East corridors is forcing higher aircraft densities into narrower airspace, amplifying collision and operational hazard risks beyond typical conflict zone concerns.

Delta Air Lines picks Amazon Leo over Starlink to power future inflight Wi-Fi

Delta Air Lines has selected Amazon Leo over SpaceX’s Starlink to power its future inflight Wi-Fi starting with…...

Delta's choice of Amazon Leo signals carrier confidence in LEO satellite technology maturation, potentially accelerating multi-carrier adoption and reducing Starlink's market dominance in aviation connectivity.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading fatal helicopter cause despite pilots' overconfidence in their reaction time, highlighting the need for enhanced detection systems and pre-flight route awareness protocols.

From concept to grand opening, SME Aviation leverages decades of experience to plan and successfully execute all types of aviation infrastructure projects.

Specialized aviation construction firms now position themselves as full-lifecycle partners, managing complex infrastructure from initial planning through operational launch rather than discrete construction phases.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Inside The Airbus A321XLR's Fuel Tank System

By adding the Rear Centre Tank, Airbus was able to increase the XLR's range to 4,700 nautical miles allowing it to fill the Boeing 757's shoes.

The rear center tank addition enables single-aisle aircraft to operate transoceanic routes previously requiring widebody jets, fundamentally reshaping fleet economics and route planning.

Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry

In two recent studies, researchers used Landsat data to fill key gaps in our knowledge of reservoir structure and dynamics.

Landsat satellite imagery enables cost-effective remote monitoring of reservoir bathymetry and water dynamics, providing critical infrastructure data for aviation route planning and emergency response operations.

NOAA Introduces Updated Icing and Turbulence Forecast

New system will provide higher-resolution weather data for aviation users.

Higher-resolution forecasts enable pilots to identify and avoid hazardous conditions with greater precision, potentially reducing weather-related incidents and improving operational efficiency.

End Of An Era: Emirates Cuts 62% Of Flights On Huge 615-Seat Airbus A380s

The world's highest-capacity double-decker layout will be disappearing, but before that, big cuts are planned...

Emirates' A380 fleet reduction signals that ultra-high-capacity aircraft economics no longer justify operations on many routes despite post-pandemic demand recovery.

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies offers the most immediate pathway to accommodate projected traffic growth while meeting environmental mandates, requiring coordinated investments across airlines, airports, and air traffic management.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Press Release: Lufthansa Technik Malta completes first 787 cabin mod

Lufthansa Technik has successfully completed its first Boeing 787 Dreamliner cabin modification at its European Center of Excellence for widebody Base Maintenance Services in Malta. This strategic achievement marks an important step for Lufthansa Technik Malta’s growing widebody service portfolio....

Lufthansa Technik Malta's 787 cabin mod capability expands European MRO competition and reduces aircraft downtime by eliminating transatlantic ferry costs for widebody interior upgrades.

The future of space warfare will be determined by the speed of decision. As satellite constellations proliferate and adversaries field increasingly sophisticated counterspace capabilities, the ability to sense, analyze and act faster than an opponent will dictate who controls the space domain....

Space warfare dominance will shift from traditional military assets to autonomous AI systems that can detect, process, and respond to threats faster than human commanders.

British Airways is the world's fifth-largest long-haul operator by number of flights. Only United Airlines, Emirates, Qatar Airways, and Delta Air Lines have more long-haul services. Between now and the end of October, BA has an average of 95 daily long-haul departures from London, up from 91...

BA's expansion to 95 daily long-haul departures positions it to challenge the top four operators' market dominance in ultra-long-haul route profitability.

More A-10s Are Headed to the Middle East

The U.S. Air Force A-10C Thunderbolt II jets have been actively supporting Operation Epic Fury in Iran...

Persistent A-10 deployments signal escalating U.S. military commitment to Iran operations, indicating close air support remains critical for regional contingencies.
